items.title: Vedanta demerger: When will four spun-off entities of the mining major trade on Dalal Street? CEO answers
items.summary: According to a PTI report, Vedanta Resources CEO Deshnee Naidoo said that its India arm will file with stock exchanges next week for listing approval of its demerged entities.

items.title: Oil firms selling petrol at ₹14 a litre loss, diesel at ₹18
items.summary: Besides losses on petrol and diesel, the elevated energy prices post West Asia crisis are likely to leave companies with an under recovery of ₹80,000 crore on cooking gas LPG in the current fiscal

items.title: Nationwide trial of mobile-based disaster alert systems going on; citizens ignore test message: DoT
items.summary: India is currently testing a new mobile-based disaster alert system. Citizens may receive test messages on their phones. These alerts are part of a nationwide trial by the Department of Telecommunications and the National Disaster Management Authority. The system uses Cell Broadcast technology for rapid dissemination of critical information.

items.title: Government proposes E100 fuel approval
items.summary: Move paves way for certification and mass production of ethanol-only vehicles

items.title: India plans to add 92 vessels with ₹51,383 crore investment in FY27
items.summary: The plan includes expanding fleets such as container vessels, LPG carriers and green tugs, alongside enhancing coordination across key ministries.

items.title: Kotak Mahindra Bank Q4 earnings preview: Brokerages see steady NII growth, muted profit expansion
items.summary: Kotak Mahindra Bank will announce its Q4FY26 results on 2 May. Brokerages expect steady growth in net interest income and operating profitability, but subdued net profit due to margin pressure. The stock has gained 8% in April, yet remains down 13% year to date.

items.title: Current growth trajectory paves way for India to be a developed nation by 2047: Jeffrey D Sachs
items.summary: Addressing an event organised by industry body Ficci, Sachs said India is one of the pillars of global stability and global peace, and it should not align with the United States.

items.title: Govt mulls partial credit guarantee, interest subvention support for pvt sector electric bus, truck adoption
items.summary: The heavy industries ministry is consulting stakeholders on partial credit guarantees and interest subsidies to make electric buses and trucks affordable for private operators, who run 90% of the country's buses but have been excluded from existing federal electric vehicle incentive schemes.

items.title: Exclusive: Casagrand to add 3.98 mn sq ft in Hyderabad, launch its second-largest residential project 'Casagrand Vybe'
items.summary: Casagrand is boosting its Hyderabad presence with 3.98 million square feet of new developments. The company is launching 'Casagrand Vybe', its largest residential project in the city. This expansion is fueled by high demand for luxury and premium homes. Hyderabad is becoming a key market for the developer, contributing significantly to its revenue. Casagrand is also preparing for its IPO.

items.title: Govt develops export monitoring framework to meet FY31 $2 trillion export target
items.summary: India aims for $2 trillion in exports by 2030-31. The Commerce Department has created a detailed plan. This framework targets sectors like engineering, textiles, and services. Key performance indicators and timelines will guide progress. Inter-departmental coordination is crucial. An IT platform will track advancements. Strengthening 'Brand India' is also a priority.

items.title: Govt deliberates on financing mechanism to push adoption of e-bus, e-truck in private sector
items.summary: The Ministry of Heavy Industries convened a high-level meeting to explore financing mechanisms for accelerating private sector adoption of electric buses and trucks. Discussions focused on identifying and addressing challenges in EV financing, with participation from financial institutions, banks, NBFCs, and industry stakeholders.

items.title: Siguler Guff invests $40 million in Cinnabon operator Trimex Foods
items.summary: US private equity firm Siguler Guff has invested $40 million in Trimex Foods, the Indian franchise partner for Chili's, Paul, and Cinnabon. This strategic investment aims to accelerate Trimex's pan-India expansion and onboard new global brands, reflecting strong conviction in India's growing organized food services sector.

items.title: Govt working on resuming Shipping Corp of India's maritime services to West Asia
items.summary: External affairs minister S. Jaishankar spoke to his Iranian counterpart Seyed Abbas Araghchi regarding the current situation in West Asia, amid reports US President Donad Trump had rejected Iran's proposal to end the war and remove Hormuz blockade.

items.title: Govt issues SOP for international flights on hub & spoke model; Air India to start trial in June
items.summary: India is set to launch a new hub and spoke aviation model. The civil aviation ministry has released standard operating procedures. Air India will begin trials in June. This system aims to connect domestic flights to international destinations smoothly. Passengers will experience easier immigration and baggage transfers. The model will also help utilize airport infrastructure across the country.

items.title: Woking on Jan Vishwas bill 3 to further improve business environment: Piyush Goyal
items.summary: India is preparing for a third Jan Vishwas Bill. This initiative aims to simplify business processes and reduce compliance burdens. The government is also focused on making it easier for businesses to start and exit. India has already finalized nine free trade agreements and expects more in the coming years. Discussions are underway for further trade pacts with various countries.

items.title: RBI eases disaster loan recast norms, mandates 5% provisioning by banks, NBFCs
items.summary: The revised rules will come into effect from 1 July 2026 and will apply prospectively, with existing restructured accounts continuing under the current framework unless a fresh plan is invoked.

items.title: Kissht IPO: OnEMI raises ₹278 crore from anchor investors ahead of issue
items.summary: The anchor round saw participation from leading domestic mutual funds and global institutions, with shares allotted at the upper price band of ₹171.

items.title: Export outlook for April positive; FTAs to drive growth: Goyal

items.title: Dr Reddy’s Laboratories receives approval from Health Canada for its generic semaglutide injection
items.summary: Dr Reddy's Laboratories has achieved a significant milestone. Health Canada has approved its generic semaglutide injection. This makes Dr Reddy's the first company to receive market authorization for this treatment in Canada. The approval covers 2 mg/pen and 4 mg/pen dosages. This development aims to increase access to affordable diabetes treatments for Canadian patients.

items.title: Nandini Piramal warns prolonged West Asia war may raise input costs
items.summary: Geopolitical tensions in West Asia are manageable now. However, a prolonged US-Israel war against Iran could raise costs for petrochemical-derived products. Piramal Pharma is diversifying sourcing and leveraging global manufacturing. The company sees improved momentum in its contract development and manufacturing business. Future growth is expected from overseas sites and complex hospital generics.

items.title: Air passenger traffic slips marginally in March
items.summary: Industry cites higher ATF prices, fuel surcharges and calibrated capacity deployment as factors shaping domestic travel trends

items.title: West Asia crisis creates a need to reprioritise policies and fiscal spending: CEA Nageswaran

items.title: PM Modi to take stock of long-term strategy to address supply chain shocks next week
items.summary: Prime Minister will meet the concerned Ministries and the empowered groups to review their long-term strategies

items.title: Bloom Energy stock hits fresh record, jumps 28% after blockbuster Q1 results and higher 2026 guidance
items.summary: Bloom Energy's shares soared 28.32% to $290.50 after a strong Q1 FY26 report, with revenues up 130.4% to $751.1 million. The company raised its full-year revenue outlook to $3.4-$3.8 billion, driven by demand from data centres and partnerships with Oracle.

items.title: India aims for $2 tn total exports by FY31 with an MSME, agri push
items.summary: The $2 trillion export target includes $1 trillion each in merchandise shipments and $1 trillion in services, the commerce ministry said.

items.title: Mauritius-based entity sells Rs 289 crore worth shares in Emcure Pharmaceuticals via block deal; Norges Bank acquirer
items.summary: Emcure Pharmaceuticals saw a significant block deal where BC Investments IV Limited sold 18 lakh shares for Rs 289 crore to Norges Bank. The pharma company, which manufactures APIs and formulations, has shown strong performance with a 67% return in the past 12 months and a 50% YoY growth in net profit.

items.title: SEBI operationalises PaRRVA to verify performance claims of market intermediaries
items.summary: After a pilot phase, full-scale operations will begin on May 4, 2026

items.title: Govt holds high-level meet to boost financing for e-buses and e-trucks
items.summary: The meeting brought together key stakeholders, including government departments, banks, NBFCs, global institutions, OEMs, and industry bodies, highlighting a coordinated push to strengthen EV financing frameworks

items.title: Brigade Group, Bain Capital JV to develop 2 million project in Bengaluru
items.summary: Brigade Group and Bain Capital are partnering for a major development in Bengaluru. This 50:50 joint venture will create a 2 million square feet mixed-use project. The investment is approximately Rs 2200 crore. The project will feature Grade A office space and a five-star hotel. It is located on ITPL Main Road, Whitefield, near the metro station.

items.title: Crude oil prices surge over 7%, extend gains to 8th day; Brent tops $119 — What's behind this sharp rally?
items.summary: US crude rose 7.6% to $107.52 a barrel, and Brent rose to $119,54 per barrel, up 7.44% after the EIA, after the US oil stocks plummeted sharply and amid little signs of resolution to the Middle East crisis that's entered its third month now.

items.title: RBI launches 3 key surveys for monetary policy input
items.summary: The RBI said it has launched the May 2026 round of the 'inflation expectations survey of households' to capture subjective assessments on price movements and inflation, based on their individual consumption baskets, across 19 cities.

items.title: FinMin asks banks to take measures for welfare of persons with disabilities
items.summary: Public sector banks and financial institutions are now required to offer reservations for individuals with benchmark disabilities in direct recruitment and promotions. The Department of Financial Services has issued clear instructions for this implementation. Furthermore, these institutions must hold regular meetings with PwD Employees Welfare Associations to address concerns and ensure their voices are heard.

items.title: Exceptional charges, client impact drag Syngene profit down 13% to ₹153 cr
items.summary: The company recognised ₹25 crore (net of tax) as exceptional expenses related to employee termination benefits, which weighed on profitability

items.title: Federal Bank goes slow on home loans to protect margins amid stiff competition
items.summary: MD &amp; CEO KVS Manian said the lender is pivoting toward higher-yielding segments and multi-product clients as thin margins make standalone housing loans less attractive.

items.title: Social justice ministry spends Rs 2,042 crore on educational schemes in FY-26
items.summary: The Union Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment has allocated over Rs 2,000 crore for educational initiatives benefiting OBC, EBC, and DNT students in the financial year 2025-26. This significant disbursement aims to enhance access to quality education and support academic progress for these communities.

items.title: Bank credit growth slows to 15% in first half of April: RBI data
items.summary: Bank credit growth saw a slowdown to 15 per cent in the fortnight ending April 15. This follows a period of rapid lending ahead of the financial year-end. Despite the recent dip, credit growth has maintained double digits for over seven months. This indicates a sustained revival in demand from both individuals and businesses.

items.title: Ahead of Market: 10 things that will decide stock market action on Thursday
items.summary: Indian stock markets saw a strong upward trend on Wednesday. The Nifty 50 and Sensex both closed higher, driven by gains in auto, FMCG, and IT sectors. Analysts observed mixed signals with key support and resistance levels identified. US and European markets traded with mixed results ahead of the Federal Reserve meeting.

items.title: Jaguar Land Rover ordered to refund Rs 1.65 crore, panel slams 'deceptive' speed claims, chassis 'surgery'
items.summary: Jaguar Land Rover India must refund 1.65 crore rupees to a Roorkee firm. The company's flagship SUV had manufacturing defects and unauthorized structural changes. The vehicle failed to meet advertised performance benchmarks. The Uttarakhand Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission ruled this an unfair trade practice. The buyer was deceived by inaccurate specifications. The firm must return the vehicle.

items.title: UAE's OPEC exit: With cartel cracked, oil spills over geopolitics
items.summary: In a dramatic turn of events, the United Arab Emirates has officially exited OPEC, signaling a significant transformation within the global energy landscape. This bold step is set to alter oil production dynamics and reshape international relationships, ushering in new energy frameworks.

items.title: MOFSL net loss widens to ₹221 crore in March quarter
items.summary: FY26 net profit drops over 25% on-year, but operating profit up 16%

items.title: RBI overhauls framework for Banks for handling loans affected by natural disasters
items.summary: A “natural calamity” is as any event recognized under official disaster relief frameworks such as the National or State Disaster Response Funds.

items.title: HUL Q4 Preview: Revenue seen at ₹16,100 crore, margins hinge on pricing strategy
items.summary: Hindustan Unilever is expected to post March quarter revenue of ₹16,100 crore and profit of ₹2,600 crore, with estimates tightly clustered. However, rising prices and a new FMCG pricing cycle are pressuring volumes, making FY27 guidance the key trigger—can demand hold up?

items.title: West Asia war, weak monsoon pose downside risk to growth, says finance ministry
items.summary: The review also noted that passing on higher energy costs to the final consumer may be inevitable. Some countries have begun to allow prices to be passed on to end-users - households and businesses - but some are yet to do so, it said.

items.title: PIB Fact Check: Petrol, diesel prices not hiked, says Centre
items.summary: PIB Fact Check: A message claiming a sharp hike in petrol and diesel prices is doing the rounds on social media. The viral post alleges that petrol prices have been increased by Rs 10 per litre and diesel by Rs 12.50, supposedly through an order issued by the Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas.

items.title: RBI clears Subrat Mohanty’s reappointment as Axis Bank Executive Director for three years
items.summary: Axis Bank announced a significant development today. The Reserve Bank of India has given its approval for Subrat Mohanty's reappointment. He will continue to serve as Executive Director for another three-year term. This new tenure is set to begin on August 17, 2026. Mohanty's extended role will conclude on August 16, 2029.

items.title: India to gain from UAE’s OPEC exit in long term
items.summary: New Delhi may gain through increased crude and LPG flows and expanded energy cooperation

items.title: Our objective is to give houses to 22,000 homebuyers of ailing Unitech: SC
items.summary: The Supreme Court is pushing for a resolution for 22,000 Unitech homebuyers. The court wants suggestions from the new board, banks, and financial institutions. Homebuyers have waited years for their dream homes. The court is investigating where homebuyers' money went. The former promoters allegedly parked funds in tax-haven countries. The court aims to ensure homes are delivered.

items.title: Andhra Pradesh natural farming initiative among 4 finalists for Food Planet Prize
items.summary: The 2026 award grants $1.5 million (approximately ₹14 crore) to the winner and $150,000 (approximately ₹1.4 crore) to each finalist

items.title: Subrat Mohanty reappointed as Axis Bank ED; RBI clears three-year extension
items.summary: His new term will run from August 17, 2026, to August 16, 2029

items.title: Piramal Pharma expects FY 2027 to see a revival in growth
items.summary: It posted a Rs ₹9 crore loss Q4 FY 26, impacted by an impairment cost of Rs ₹176 crore linked to assets under development

items.title: Global consumer products makers reported strong growth momentum in India in March quarter
items.summary: This comes at a time when consumer products players are facing headwinds due to geopolitical tensions and uncertain macroeconomic conditions, which has impacted global supply chains.

items.title: ADB cuts growth outlook of Asia to 4.7% as West Asia disruptions deepen
items.summary: The Asian Development Bank has lowered its 2026 economic growth forecast for Asia and the Pacific. Prolonged West Asia disruptions are driving up energy prices and tightening financial conditions. Inflation projections have also been significantly raised. The ADB warns of systemic, long-lasting disruptions to global energy and trade networks. The bank will support the region's economy and track fast-moving risks.

items.title: Cognizant to cut 4,000 jobs as AI push, weak demand weigh on outlook
items.summary: Cognizant’s Project Leap restructuring targets $300 million in savings and margin expansion, even as the company lowers full-year guidance amid a softer demand environment.

items.title: Adani Green Energy to add up to 15,000 GWh of battery energy storage capacity annually
items.summary: Adani Green Energy plans massive battery storage expansion. The company will add thousands of gigawatt hours of capacity each year. This move supports India's energy transition. It aims to provide reliable clean power. The expansion will involve significant investment. This will help meet peak demand. It ensures round-the-clock renewable energy supply.

items.title: India moves to ratify 100% ethanol as vehicle fuel under motor vehicle rules
items.summary: The draft amendment to Central Motor Vehicle Rules aims to allow vehicles to run on up to 100% ethanol and fully biodiesel fuels, as the government looks to absorb surplus supply and deepen its biofuel push.

items.title: Vedanta's demerged entities to trade by mid-June after split, says CEO
items.summary: Mining giant Vedanta is set to file for listing approval of its demerged companies next week. Shares of these new entities are anticipated to begin trading by mid-June. This strategic move will create five independent, sector-specific businesses. Each company will pursue its own growth path and attract investors.

items.title: India plans 62 vessels in FY27 with Rs 51,383 crore investment: Union minister Sarbananda Sonowal
items.summary: India is accelerating its shipping capabilities to achieve self-reliance, with plans to add 62 vessels by FY 2026-27, backed by a significant investment. A high-level meeting emphasized expanding the fleet, including container vessels, LPG and crude carriers, and green tugs, to ensure resilience against global disruptions and sustain supply chains.

items.title: Poll of Exit Polls 2026: Close contest in West Bengal, NDA leads in Assam; Tamil Nadu turns multi-cornered, Kerala signals shift, NDA edges ahead in Puducherry
items.summary: Poll of Exit Polls 2026 shows a close BJP–TMC contest in West Bengal, while the NDA is projected to retain Assam with a clear lead. Tamil Nadu sees a multi-cornered fight, Kerala hints at a possible shift towards UDF, and NDA is ahead in Puducherry.

items.title: Asia, Europe could see jet fuel shortages in coming months: IATA chief Willie Walsh
items.summary: Global airlines' grouping IATA warns of potential jet fuel shortages in Asia and Europe in the coming months.  Extraordinarily high fuel costs are increasingly reflected in ticket prices.  This situation is testing airline resilience.  Regulators are urged to grant flexibility on slots due to extraordinary circumstances.  March traffic and forward bookings remain unaffected so far.

items.title: Domestic air traffic rises to 4.37 cr in March quarter; flight delays hit 1.21 lakh passengers last month
items.summary: In March this year, the air traffic inched up to 1.44 crore from 1.41 crore in February, but was lower than the 1.45 crore recorded in March 2025.

items.title: Market Trading Guide: Buy RIL and Coal India on Thursday for near term gains up to 8%
items.summary: Nifty's technical indicators suggest potential for a bullish crossover. Analysts recommend buying Reliance Industries and Coal India. Reliance Industries shows strong bullish momentum with potential upside to Rs 1,500-1,520. Coal India broke resistance and is poised for gains towards Rs 510-520.

items.title: Air India Express expected to post FY26 loss of around ₹3,500 cr
items.summary: Board to review FY26 financials amid rising fuel costs, and ongoing network expansion

items.title: Bajaj Finance sees AI-led gains for customers, staff in FY27
items.summary: Bajaj Finance posted a 22% jump in Q4 profit to  ₹5,553 crore and crossed  ₹5 trillion in AUM, as AI-driven efficiencies, including a sixfold rise in peak-day loan processing, began showing up in operations.

items.title: LANXESS invests Rs 150 cr to set up new lubricant additives business facility in Gujarat's Jhagadia
items.summary: Specialty chemicals maker LANXESS is investing Rs 150 crore in Gujarat. A new blending plant will be set up at its Jhagadia site. This facility will produce specialty lubricant additives. It will serve customers in India, the Middle East, and other global markets. The plant reinforces LANXESS's commitment to regional customers.

items.title: Sterling Tools FY26 revenue at ₹800 crore; mobility delays, ₹200 crore capex plan on
items.summary: Jaideep Wadhwa, Managing Director of the EV arm, Sterling E-Mobility Ltd, said FY26 revenue would be lower than the previous year, attributing the decline to a timing mismatch in programme ramp-ups.

items.summary: Separately, the Commerce Minister added that it was also important for corporates to have economically-viable propositions

items.title: Auto LPG sales double in April; domestic supply remains stable despite West Asia crisis: Centre
items.summary: Auto LPG sales in the country recorded a surge of nearly 100 per cent in April compared to the start of the year, even as the government maintained steady domestic supplies despite the ongoing geopolitical crisis in West Asia.

items.summary: Consumers deferred their jewellery purchases due to volatile prices and opted instead to invest in instruments such as gold exchange traded funds

items.title: Mindspace Business Parks REIT records 7.1 million sq ft leasing in FY26
items.summary: Mindspace REIT achieved significant leasing of over 7.1 million sq ft in 2025-26. The March quarter saw robust income growth and a record distribution of Rs 6.64 per unit. Occupancy rose to 95.7%, driven by strong demand from global firms. The REIT is also expanding its Chennai portfolio, reinforcing its market position.

items.title: Trump's US Fed chief nominee Kevin Warsh clears key confirmation hurdle in Senate
items.summary: The Senate Banking Committee voted along party lines to advance Warsh's nomination to the full Republican-controlled Senate. All 13 Republicans on the panel supported Warsh.

items.summary: On expiry of derivatives contract, investors can trigger for gold delivery and it will be delivered to the buyers address

items.title: Why more Pune homebuyers are returning to furniture stores before buying
items.summary: Pune residents are increasingly visiting Wooden Street furniture stores, shifting from online browsing to in-person experiences. This move stems from a desire to better assess furniture's fit, feel, and functionality within their homes, leading to more confident and practical purchasing decisions.

items.title: Armed with Govt missive, India’s APEDA poised to take up rice GMO issue with China
items.summary: Environment Ministry, ICAR deny GM-rice  is cultivated in India in communication to the export promotion authority

items.title: Zee Media AI Exit Poll 2026: BJP edge in West Bengal, hat-trick in Assam; DMK set for big win in Tamil Nadu; close fight in Kerala, NDA ahead in Puducherry
items.summary: Zee Media AI Exit Poll 2026 projects a tight contest in West Bengal with a slight BJP edge, while the BJP alliance is set for a hat-trick win in Assam. DMK alliance is heading for a big win in Tamil Nadu, Kerala remains a close fight, and NDA is likely ahead in Puducherry.

items.title: RBI allows banks to extend relief measures to borrowers without their requests in disaster-hit areas
items.summary: New Reserve Bank of India guidelines empower banks to offer relief to all borrowers in calamity-hit regions. These measures, effective July 1, 2026, can be extended without borrower requests. Banks can also operate from temporary premises and set up satellite offices. ATM services will be restored promptly. Relief can include fee waivers for up to a year.

items.title: US stocks today: Fed chief nominee Warsh clears key hurdle in Senate confirmation process
items.summary: Kevin Warsh's nomination to lead the Federal Reserve advanced through the Senate Banking Committee along party lines. This move comes as President Trump seeks to exert more control over the central bank.

items.title: West Asia conflict raises inflation risks; India's domestic strength offers cushion: Finmin report
items.summary: India faces supply shocks from the West Asia conflict. This impacts inflation and trade. However, strong domestic demand and policy measures provide resilience. The economy is forecast to grow robustly. Government actions aim to manage costs in key sectors. Trade performance is being boosted by new schemes and agreements. India is positioned to leverage global shifts for economic gains.

items.title: Ahead of IPO, founders invest  ₹40 crore in digital lending platform Kissht at premium
items.summary: Founders Ranvir Singh and Krishnan Vishwanathan have collectively invested at  ₹201 per share, higher than the IPO price band of  ₹162-171, signalling confidence in the business, they added.

items.title: Google, Alphabet to Meta: 4 tech mega-caps among ~270 US companies set to post March quarter results today
items.summary: Among the mega-caps slated to report January-March quarter results are Alphabet, Microsoft, Amazon, and Meta Platforms. Overall, 269 companies will post their quarterly earnings.

items.title: Indiabulls Q4 Results: Company registers 194 crore profit supported by higher income
items.summary: Indiabulls Limited reports FY26 revenue of Rs 880 crore and profit of Rs 346 crore, with Q4FY26 revenue at Rs 418 crore and a real estate pipeline of over Rs 21,000 crore.

items.title: How university-edtech collaborations are contributing to building India’s AI-ready workforce
items.summary: Indian universities are evolving higher education through collaboration with edtech platforms to address the employability gap among STEM graduates. Partnerships like those with Simplilearn embed practitioner-led content and outcome-linked assessments directly into curricula, preparing students for AI-driven careers and meeting accreditation standards.

items.title: Gold, silver rates today: Comex gold slides $86/oz, silver drops $1.5/oz ahead of US Fed decision, oil surge
items.summary: Precious metals continued to decline, with gold futures dropping $86 to $4,522 and silver futures falling $1.5 to $71.71. Rising crude oil prices heighten inflation concerns, prompting expectations of sustained higher interest rates from central banks and impacting the appeal of these metals.

items.title: WhatsApp launches prepaid mobile recharge feature in India: Here’s how it works
items.summary: Meta-owned messaging platform WhatsApp has introduced a new feature in India that allows users to complete prepaid mobile recharges directly within the app. The feature is being introduced in phases across Android and iOS devices and will become available to all users over the coming weeks.

items.title: US stocks today: Fed likely to hold rates steady at what may be last meeting of Powell era
items.summary: The Federal Reserve is poised to maintain interest rates unchanged, grappling with rising inflation concerns fueled by escalating oil prices. Policymakers are debating whether to signal a prolonged period of steady rates or even potential hikes. This meeting marks a potential end to Jerome Powell's tenure as Fed chief, with Kevin Warsh expected to take over.

items.title: RBI rejects NBFC pleas on funds, norms tightening
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has rejected several suggestions from Non-Banking Finance Companies. Equity investments in group entities will not be excluded from indirect public funds. Borrowings from promoters and related parties will be treated as public funds. Intra-group loans will be considered customer interface. These decisions are part of final directions for small NBFCs.

items.title: Vedanta Q4 net profit up 89% on rally in metal prices, cost efficiency
items.summary: Revenue jumped 29 per cent to ₹51,524 crore (₹39,789 crore). Ebitda in Q4 increased 59 per cent to ₹18,447 crore (₹11,618 crore) on back of higher realisation in both zinc and aluminium business besides rupee depreciation against dollar

items.title: Broker’s call: Maruti Suzuki (Buy)
items.summary: Emkay Global

items.title: Indian Bank Q4 profit rises 5% to Rs 2,956 crore
items.summary: Indian Bank announced a 5% increase in net profit for the fourth quarter. The bank saw its operating profit rise and its gross and net non-performing assets fall significantly. Despite challenges foreseen in treasury operations due to global events, the bank projects steady growth in advances for the current fiscal year. Deposits also showed robust growth.

items.title: Force Motors FY26 revenue up 12.2% at ₹9,056 crore; Q4 profit down 36%
items.summary: Standalone net profit for the March quarter fell 36 per cent % year-on-year to ₹273.7 crore, compared with ₹429.6 crore in the year-ago period, largely due to the absence of exceptional gains recorded last year.

items.title: Rupee closes at record low of 94.84 amid rising oil prices, geopolitical uncertainty linked to West Asia conflict
items.summary: The central bank may turn more aggressive in the market if the rupee breaches the psychologically crucial 95 mark

items.title: Nithin Kamath on why retail investors' participation in auction trades is hitting prop desks
items.summary: Nithin Kamath of Zerodha reports that opening stock exchange auction markets to retail investors is reshaping dynamics, impacting proprietary trading firms. Increased retail participation has improved liquidity and narrowed spreads in these auctions, which historically offered easy profits for specialized traders. This shift is making the market more efficient.

items.title: Domestic air traffic shrinks in March as West Asia war impact reaches Indian skies
items.summary: One reason for the decline in traffic is the reduced connecting traffic. India has limited international airports, and the traffic moves on domestic flights to the final destination. A reduction in flights to the Gulf and cancellation of trips by many passengers also had an impact.

items.title: Union Bank Under Scanner: Senior official flags CASA irregularities in RBI complaint
items.summary: RBI complaint raises serious questions over Union Bank’s FY26 CASA surge and sharp post-year-end reversal amid allegations of window dressing and unusual account activity.

items.title: Exclusive: RBI gold monetisation scheme — earn from idle gold at home, rules explained
items.summary: Exclusive: Gold monetisation scheme revamp may allow households to earn from idle gold at home, with new proposals to make the scheme simpler and more attractive. With over 20,000 tonnes of gold lying unused, policymakers and industry bodies are pushing reforms to reduce imports and turn gold into a productive asset.

items.title: DP World scales up inland transport to support India-UAE trade
items.summary: Shipping lines are operating services to ports like Khor Fakkan, Fujairah, Sohar and Salalah as the Strait of Hormuz remains blocked

items.title: Indian Bank net rises 11 per cent y-o-y in FY26 on growth in core business
items.summary: Binod Kumar, MD and CEO, Indian Bank, told mediapersons in an interaction that the public sector lender has exceeded guidance for FY26 on all operational parameters
